title: Pluto / StreamlinePro
subtitle: Multi-tenant booking platform — workflows between app, API, and integrations

packages.name: Expo App
packages.description: The same codebase ships to iOS, Android, and web via react-native-web.

packages.name: Stripe Connect
packages.description: Connected (Express) accounts for companies + workers; webhooks flip onboarding status.

packages.name: S3
packages.description: Filesystem default disk — used for temp + final booking photos.

packages.name: EAS Build
packages.description: Expo Application Services — native iOS/Android binaries, configured in app/eas.json.

packages.name: Web Bundle (nginx)
packages.description: Static expo-export bundle served from /var/www/plutoapp-dist by nginx.

flows.name: User login
flows.description: Mobile/web user authenticates against the Laravel API and receives a Sanctum personal access token.

steps.label: POST /api/v1/auth/login
steps.note: Throttled to 30/min per IP at the route layer (api.php:52).

steps.label: SELECT user + roles
steps.note: PasswordAuditService also writes a hit/miss row for rate-limit + anomaly detection.

steps.label: 200 OK
steps.note: Mobile stores the token; subsequent requests carry it as `Authorization: Bearer …`.

flows.name: Invite new user (temp-password handoff)
flows.description: Admin/Manager creates a teammate account. There is no magic-link email — the API returns a temporary password in the response that the admin shares out-of-band.

steps.label: POST /api/v1/users
steps.note: Triggered from Settings → Team → Add user. UserController::store; gated to actors with an application role or 'Manager'.

steps.label: INSERT users + role assignment
steps.payload: User{ id, email, company_id, password=Hash(temp), is_active }; pivot row on roles via Spatie Permission
steps.note: Manager scope is forced to actor.company_id; Application-role actors can pick company_id.

steps.label: 201 Created
steps.note: `meta.temp_password` is only present when the caller did NOT supply a password. Admin reads it out-of-band to the new user. There is no automated invitation email.

flows.name: Create a booking
flows.description: Dispatcher creates a job. Insert is transactional via BookingService::createForCompany; no email/notification is sent at this stage — that happens on dispatch (see next flow).

steps.label: POST /api/v1/bookings
steps.note: BookingController::store delegates to BookingService::createForCompany inside a DB transaction.

steps.label: INSERT bookings + booking_workers + booking_items (transactional)
steps.note: Late-assign notification only fires if the booking is already Dispatched (see BookingService.php:553).

flows.name: Dispatch booking → customer email
flows.description: Once the booking has a billable product, an appointment slot, an address, and at least one worker, the dispatcher transitions it to 'Dispatched'. This is what triggers the customer-facing confirmation email.

steps.label: POST /api/v1/bookings/{id}/dispatch
steps.note: BookingController::dispatch → BookingService::dispatch.

steps.label: UPDATE bookings SET status='Dispatched'
steps.note: Guarded: validates billable product, appointment, address, ≥1 assigned worker (BookingService.php:597).

steps.label: EmailService::sendBookingEmail(booking, 'booking_dispatched')
steps.note: Synchronous send, not queued. Independent of worker emails (BookingService.php:642).

flows.name: Upload a job photo
flows.description: Worker captures a photo with expo-image-picker. The API only stores it in S3 temp-space and returns the path — the path is later written into bookings.photos (a JSON column) when the booking is saved.

steps.label: POST /api/v1/bookings/upload-photo (multipart)
steps.note: BookingController::uploadPhoto delegates to PhotoUploadService::storeTemp.

steps.label: putObject(temp/bookings/{uuid}.{ext})
steps.note: Streamed via Laravel's default filesystem disk (S3 in prod).

steps.label: 201 Created
steps.note: Mobile holds the path; sends it back later as part of the booking PUT. PhotoUploadService::moveToBooking then promotes temp → permanent path and the value lands in bookings.photos (JSON array).

flows.name: Stripe Connect onboarding
flows.description: Worker (or company owner) connects a Stripe Express account so they can receive payouts. The webhook is what flips onboarding to 'verified'.

steps.label: POST /api/v1/stripe/onboarding/worker
steps.note: User taps 'Connect Stripe' on the Earnings screen. (api.php:402)

steps.label: accounts.create + accountLinks.create
steps.note: StripeOnboardingController.php:141, :162.

steps.label: 200 OK
steps.note: Mobile opens the URL in an in-app browser.

steps.label: Webhook: account.updated
steps.note: Signature-verified by StripeWebhookController (line 115-226). Flips status to 'verified' once charges_enabled = true.

flows.name: Customer review (token-gated)
flows.description: When a booking flips to 'Completed', the Booking model observer queues SendReviewRequestJob, which emails the customer a tokenized link. The customer fills it out without logging in.

steps.label: Booking::booted() observer: status→'Completed'
steps.note: Booking.php:37 — wasChanged('status') && status === 'Completed' → SendReviewRequestJob::dispatch($id).

steps.label: EmailService → 'How did we do?' email
steps.note: SendReviewRequestJob.php:54.

steps.label: Customer clicks the signed link
steps.note: Opens https://app/reviews/{token}.

steps.label: GET /api/v1/public/reviews/{token}
steps.note: Throttled 60/min per IP (api.php:77). Returns booking summary + form config.

flows.name: Native release — eas build (manual)
flows.description: There is no `eas build` script in app/package.json — it's run ad-hoc against the profiles in app/eas.json (development / preview / production).

steps.label: eas build --profile production --platform all
steps.note: Reads app/eas.json. Production profile uses channel='production', autoIncrement=true.

steps.label: expo prebuild + native compile (iOS & Android)
steps.note: Runs on EAS-hosted macOS/Linux workers. appVersionSource='remote' — version is managed by EAS.

steps.label: eas submit → App Store Connect / Play Console
steps.note: Manual / out-of-CI. Gated on a successful build.

steps.label: TestFlight / Play internal track
steps.note: Smoke-tested before promoting to production.

flows.name: Web deploy — pluto-pull.sh
flows.description: Same Expo codebase exported as a static web bundle and rsynced to nginx. Driven by deploy/pluto-pull.sh on the server (CD-pull style).

steps.label: npx expo export --platform web
steps.note: Equivalent to `npm run build:web` (EXPO_UNSTABLE_TREE_SHAKING=true expo export -p web --output-dir dist).

steps.label: rsync dist/ → /var/www/plutoapp-dist
steps.note: deploy/pluto-pull.sh:100. nginx serves the static bundle from there.

steps.label: EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL → /api/v1
steps.note: Web bundle calls the same API as native via CORS-allowed origin.
